title: asperula aspera
intro: Asperula aspera, also known as Wood Sorrel or Sharp-leaved Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Asperula aspera include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as possessing antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Asperula aspera can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which is often used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
